Q5 NAT Not checked

NAT
Correct answer: 261
Solution

Q6 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • Beginner • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Six persons A, B, C, D, E and F are standing in a row. C and D are standing close to each other alongside E. B is standing beside A only. A is fourth from F. Who are standing on the extremes?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
As per data given,
CDE will be together in some order i.e.
CDE or ECD or DCE or EDC
Since, A is fourth from F, the possible combinations are
F _ _ _ A_ or _F _ _ _ A
or
Since E, C and D are together and B is besides A, the possible combination is F(ECD)AB.
Thus, B and F have to be at extremes.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
For 8-way set associative cache, 3 more bits will be required in the TAG as compared to direct mapping, since 23 = 8.

Q65 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are true?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
In cycle stealing DMA transfer, the DMA controller requests only one or two memory cycles from the CPU at a time. If the CPU is trying to access memory during that time, it will wait.
Otherwise, CPU can continue with its internal operation that does not require access to the memory bus.
So CPU and DMA cycles can be overlapped. Cycle stealing mode is suitable for large block transfers since the overhead will be high. So, small block preferable. For block mode, large blocks are preferable.
